title: Than Heaven more remote, by Emily Dickinson — Deep Poetry Lines
content:
Than Heaven more remote,
For Heaven is the root,
But these the flitted seed.
More flown indeed
Than ones that never were,
Or those that hide, and are.

What madness, by their side,
A vision to provide
Of future days
They cannot praise.

My soul, to find them, come,
They cannot call, they're dumb,
Nor prove, nor woo,
But that they have abode
Is absolute as God,
And instant, too.
meaning: "Than Heaven more remote," by Emily Dickinson is a deep poem consisting of 18 lines. The poet explores themes of deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Than Heaven more remote, For Heaven is the root,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Emily Dickinson's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
